channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Iran’s IRGC says spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini killed in US-Israeli attack
item.description: Israeli and US air attacks pound Iran as assassination campaign of country's leadership continues.

item.title: Israel says it hit Syrian army camps in the south after Druze ‘attacked’
item.description: Israeli air strikes target army camps in response to alleged attacks on the Druze community in Suwayda on Thursday.

item.title: Refugee Council questions Australia’s Iran strategy at Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: Women's Asian Cup hosts Australia face criticism for the political tug of war over the Iranian women's football team.

item.title: Iran today, Africa tomorrow
item.description: What is being done to Iran today could be done to Africa tomorrow. Africa must reject this illegal war.

item.title: Iran does not need to close the Strait of Hormuz to disrupt it
item.description: The real risk lies not in closure, but in disruption in the strait’s approaches.

item.title: Iran developing a ‘vetting system’ for Strait of Hormuz transit: Report
item.description: Lloyd's List reports that ships could be allowed to pass through 'safe corridor' once they are approved by IRGC.

item.title: Eid celebrations dimmed by war and displacement across Middle East
item.description: Economic crises and conflict dampen traditional Eid celebrations in Iran, Gaza, and Lebanon.

item.title: Five problems the Iran war could solve for Israel’s Netanyahu
item.description: From the Iranian threat to his own domestic legal and political troubles, war with Iran has advantages for Netanyahu.

item.title: Iran hits Kuwaiti oil refinery as Israel renews attacks on Tehran
item.description: Attacks stretch from Kuwait to the UAE as analysts warn conflict is entering a dangerous new phase.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 21 of US-Israel attacks?
item.description: Tehran has warned of zero restraint if energy facilities are attacked again, while Netanyahu points to ground operation.
